title: caltha palustris subsp. cornuta
intro: Caltha palustris subsp. cornuta, also known as marsh marigold, is a perennial flowering plant that belongs to the buttercup family. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help in treating skin conditions and wounds. The plant is rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been found to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, contributing to its health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, marsh marigold is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to take advantage of its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
